LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 25, 2018 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF TIM CHEVALIER AN OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND IN WRITING TO THE ARGUMENTS MADE IN GOOGLE'S REPLY MEMORANDUM THAT: 1) MR. CHEVALIER IS NOT SEEKING A PUBLIC INJUNCTION; 2) THE PARTIES' ARBITRATION AGREEMENT DOES NOT BAR THE ARBITRATOR FROM ISSUING A PUBLIC INJUNCTION; AND 3) EVEN IF MR. CHEVALIER WAS SEEKING A PUBLIC INJUNCTION , MR. CHEVALIER'S REQUEST FOR A PUBLIC INJUNCTION SHOULD BE SEVERED FROM THE REMAINING ARBITRABLE ISSUES AND STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION. MR. CHEVALIER MUST FILE HIS SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION MEMORANDUM BY SEPTEMBER 12, 2018. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Continued To Apr-10-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant Google LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending conclusion of arbitration proceedings between the parties. The parties agree that the broad arbitration clause at paragraph 12 of the parties' employment agreement applies to the claims alleged by plaintiff Tim Chevalier in his complaint, albeit they disagree whether the clause covers the injunctive relief sought by Mr. Chevalier. Mr. Chevalier's defense to enforcement of the arbitration agreement that it illegally prevents public injunctive relief lacks merit. Mr. Chevalier is not seeking a public injunction. "Relief that has the primary purpose or effect of redressing or preventing injury to an individual plaintiff-or to a group of individuals similarly situated to the plaintiff-does not constitute public injunctive relief." (McGill v. Citibank, N.A. (2017) 2 Cal.5th 945.) The allegations of the complaint show that Mr. Chevalier is seeking private injunctive relief. (Complaint, pars. 77, 86, 96-99; prayer, par. 2.) The injunction that Mr. Chevalier seeks will benefit him and similarly situated Google employees. It will not affect the public at large. Nor does the arbitration agreement bar the arbitrator from issuing a public injunction. The agreement provides that Mr. Chevalier may only pursue claims on an "individual basis" and waives his right to commence a "collective action" against Google "to the fullest extent permitted by law." It also provides that Mr. Chevalier is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any remedies "available under applicable law." Courts interpret contracts in a fashion that render them enforceable. (Pearson Dental Supplies, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 665, 682; CC 3541; CC 1643.) Such an interpretation would allow Mr. Chevalier to seek a public injunction before the arbitrator. The arbitration agreement in McGill did not include the similar qualifying language.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Sep-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Google, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is continued to September 25, 2018 to give plaintiff Tim Chevalier an opportunity to respond in writing to the arguments made in Google's reply memorandum that: 1) Mr. Chevalier is not seeking a public injunction; 2) the parties' arbitration agreement does not bar the arbitrator from issuing a public injunction; and 3) even if Mr. Chevalier was seeking a public injunction , Mr. Chevalier's request for a public injunction should be severed from the remaining arbitrable issues and stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. Mr. Chevalier must file his supplemental opposition memorandum by September 12, 2018.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)
